title: Aussie Lunchbox Planner

onboarding_text: Lunchbox sorted — healthy meals your kids will actually eat.
home_feat1_desc: AI-powered meal plans that follow NHMRC Australian Dietary Guidelines — so every lunchbox is balanced without the guesswork.
home_feat2_desc: See real-time Woolworths prices and Coles estimates side-by-side. Know exactly what your weekly shop will cost before you leave the house.
home_feat3_desc: From breakfast prep to the school gate — your full week of lunches is planned before your morning coffee gets cold.

about_subtitle: Built by Australian parents who got tired of the Sunday-night lunchbox scramble.
about_feat1_desc: Tell us your family's allergies and hit Generate. In five seconds flat, you'll have a full Monday-to-Friday lineup with recipes, ingredients, and a printable shopping list.
about_feat2_desc: Every ingredient shows a live Woolworths price alongside a Coles estimate, so you can compare costs before leaving the house and stick to your budget.
about_feat3_desc: Meals follow the Australian Dietary Guidelines published by the National Health and Medical Research Council, balancing the five food groups across every weekly plan.
about_community_desc: Thousands of Aussie parents already use the planner each week. Share your feedback, suggest recipes, and help us build something better for every family.
contact_subtitle: Got a question, spotted a bug, or want to collaborate? Drop a line — I read every message.
contact_desc: Whether it's about a recipe, a pricing glitch, or an idea for a new feature, I'm always happy to chat.
contact_location: Built in Auckland, NZ — for Australian families
contact_hours: I aim to respond within 48 hours.
contact_support_tip: 💡 Reporting a technical issue? Attach your browser name, version, and a screenshot so we can track it down faster.
contact_success_msg: Thanks for reaching out — I'll be in touch soon.
contact_error: Looks like some required fields are empty.
contact_editorial_desc: Spotted a mistake in an article or recipe? Let me know the title and the specific error — I review corrections within two business days.
faq_subtitle: Answers to the most common questions from Aussie parents using the planner.
faq_still_desc: I personally reply to every message — shoot me a note and I'll sort it out.
faq_a1: Not a cent. The full planner — meal generation, recipes, shopping lists, PDF downloads — is free and always will be. If we add optional extras down the track, the core tool stays open to everyone.
faq_q2: What happens when I hit Generate?
faq_a2: The engine scans our database of 100+ Australian-tested recipes and picks five balanced lunches spanning different categories — sandwiches, hot meals, baked goods, and cold options. It respects every allergy and dietary filter you've ticked, then builds a categorised shopping list with live Woolworths pricing.
faq_q3: Which allergy and dietary filters are available?
faq_a3: Six filters are available: Nut-Free, Dairy-Free, Gluten-Free, Egg-Free, Vegetarian, and Vegan. Toggle any combination before generating, and the planner automatically excludes recipes containing those allergens or animal products.
faq_q4: Can I fully trust the allergy filters?
faq_a4: Every recipe in our database is individually tagged and reviewed for allergens. That said, the products you buy at the supermarket may contain traces due to shared manufacturing lines, and formulations can change without notice. For any allergy — especially anaphylaxis-level — always read the product label, even on items you've bought before.
faq_q5: How are the supermarket prices sourced?
faq_a5: Woolworths prices come from live data feeds and are refreshed every one to two weeks. Coles figures are estimates based on recent pricing history. ALDI is not currently included. Treat all figures as a planning guide — your local store may vary.
faq_q6: Where do the calorie numbers come from?
faq_a6: Nutritional data is drawn from the Australian Food Composition Database maintained by FSANZ. These are approximate values based on standard serve sizes, useful for general guidance but not precise enough for clinical diets.
faq_q7: Is there a way to save plans I like?
faq_a7: Sign in with Google (free) and any plan you generate is automatically saved to your account. You can revisit, re-download, or re-email it at any time.
faq_q8: How do I get the shopping list out of the app?
faq_a8: After generating a plan, tap 'Download Plan PDF' — the file includes the full menu plus a shopping list sorted by supermarket aisle with price estimates. You can also email it directly or share via WhatsApp.
faq_q9: Our school is nut-free. Will the planner respect that?
faq_a9: Absolutely. Switch on the Nut-Free filter and every suggested recipe will be free of peanuts and tree nuts. We still recommend double-checking product labels, as manufacturing processes can change.
faq_q10: Is the app available in other languages?
faq_a10: Three languages are supported right now: English, Korean (한국어), and Chinese (中文). Switch between them using the language toggle in the navigation bar.
faq_q11: I'd like to suggest a recipe or flag an error.
faq_a11: We're all ears. Head to the Contact page, pick the relevant topic, and include as much detail as possible. Recipe ideas, allergy-tag corrections, and pricing errors are all welcome — we act on every report.
faq_q12: What does the Fridge Leftovers field do?
faq_a12: Type an ingredient you already have — say, 'chicken' or 'sweet potato' — into the Fridge Leftovers box on the sidebar. The planner will then favour recipes that use that ingredient, helping you cut waste and save money.
faq_q13: Can I use it on my phone?
faq_a13: The entire site is responsive. Generate plans, browse recipes, and download PDFs on any smartphone or tablet — no app install needed.
faq_q14: How fresh is the recipe collection?
faq_a14: New recipes land roughly every month after going through our full test-and-review process. Supermarket prices are refreshed every one to two weeks, and seasonal tweaks happen at the start of each school term.
faq_q15: Walk me through creating my first plan.
faq_a15: Open the Planner page — no account required. On the left sidebar (or tap the filter icon on mobile), set any allergy filters your family needs, exclude ingredients your child won't eat, and optionally type a fridge leftover you'd like to use up. Press 'Generate Plan' and within about five seconds you'll see a complete Monday-to-Friday lunchbox schedule. Each day shows a main meal with ingredients, Woolworths and Coles price estimates, and a snack suggestion. From there you can download everything as a single PDF or email the shopping list to yourself. The whole process takes under sixty seconds.
faq_q16: What if I don't like one of the suggested meals?
faq_a16: Hit Generate again — each run picks a fresh combination from over 100 recipes, so the result will be different every time. To nudge the planner toward meals you prefer, add favoured ingredients in the 'Fridge Leftovers' field or exclude ones your child dislikes. A day-level swap feature that lets you replace a single day without regenerating the full week is on the roadmap for late 2026.
faq_q17: My child has multiple allergies. Can I stack filters?
faq_a17: Stack as many as you need. All six filters — Nut-Free, Dairy-Free, Gluten-Free, Egg-Free, Vegetarian, Vegan — work in any combination. If the resulting recipe pool is too small for a full five-day plan, the planner will let you know so you can decide whether to relax a filter.
faq_q18: How does the planner deal with cross-contamination?
faq_a18: Recipes are tagged strictly by their ingredient lists — no nuts in the recipe means a nut-free tag. What we can't control is cross-contamination during commercial food production or in your own kitchen. If your child is at risk of anaphylaxis, always read the label on every product (even ones you've bought before — formulations change), and consult your child's allergist or immunologist for personalised guidance. The planner is a meal-planning aid, not a medical tool.
faq_q19: Is the planner safe for a coeliac child?
faq_a19: The Gluten-Free filter excludes recipes containing wheat, barley, rye, and non-certified oats — covering the primary gluten sources. Because coeliac disease demands zero tolerance for trace gluten, we recommend treating our filter as a starting point and always confirming that the brands you purchase carry a certified gluten-free label. Coeliac Australia (coeliac.org.au) publishes a regularly updated product directory that's worth bookmarking.
faq_q20: Any tips for keeping lunchbox costs down?
faq_a20: Start with the 'Fridge Leftovers' feature — using ingredients already in your kitchen can shave $5–10 off the weekly bill. Next, glance at the Woolworths vs Coles price column in your shopping list and pick whichever store is cheaper for staples like bread, eggs, and pasta. Sunday batch-cooking (a big pasta bake, a tray of scrolls) slashes the per-serve cost dramatically. Finally, buy fruit and veg in season — it's cheaper, tastier, and aligns with NHMRC dietary advice. Check our blog for a full budget breakdown. Most families land between $12 and $17 per child per week.
faq_q21: Are specials and loyalty discounts factored into prices?
faq_a21: No — we use regular shelf prices from Woolworths, updated every one to two weeks. Weekly specials and Everyday Rewards or Flybuys discounts change too fast and vary by region, so we don't track them. The upside is that our figures are effectively a ceiling: you'll almost always spend less once you factor in current deals. Pro tip: open the Woolworths and Coles apps the night before your shop, compare what's on special, and tweak your plan accordingly.
faq_q22: Can the planner produce a two-week shopping list?
faq_a22: Right now each run covers one week. For a fortnightly shop, generate two plans back-to-back and merge the shopping lists. A strategy many parents use: buy shelf-stable items (tinned goods, rice, crackers) for both weeks in a single trip, then grab fresh produce (bread, fruit, deli meats) at the start of week two. It cuts shopping trips in half without sacrificing freshness.
faq_q23: What makes the meals nutritionally balanced?
faq_a23: Every recipe is mapped to the five food groups in the Australian Dietary Guidelines (NHMRC) — grains, vegetables and legumes, fruit, lean protein, and dairy or alternatives. The generator distributes variety across these groups over five days so no single category dominates. Each lunch targets a protein source, a wholegrain or starchy component, and at least one fruit or vegetable serve. For children with clinical dietary needs, we always recommend working alongside an Accredited Practising Dietitian.
faq_q24: Will the portions suit my child's age?
faq_a24: Recipes target primary-school children aged 5–12, with each lunch sitting in the 300–450 calorie range — appropriate for the average child in that bracket. Younger kids (Prep to Year 2) may not finish everything, while older or sporty kids (Year 5–6) might need an extra snack or a bigger serve. The best guide is your own child: if food regularly comes home uneaten, scale back; if they're ravenous after school, add a piece of fruit or a muesli bar.
faq_q25: How accurate is the nutritional information?
faq_a25: Calorie and macronutrient figures come from the Australian Food Composition Database published by Food Standards Australia New Zealand (FSANZ). They're calculated against standard portion sizes and are intended to give parents a rough sense of energy content — not clinical-grade precision. Real-world values will shift depending on the specific brands and quantities you use at home.
faq_q26: How can I share a plan with my partner or co-parent?
faq_a26: Download the plan as a PDF — it bundles the 5-day menu, every recipe, and the full shopping list into a single file you can AirDrop, email, or send through any messaging app. If you're signed in, you can also tap 'Email Plan' to fire a copy straight from the planner. Shared family accounts are on our radar — tell us via the Contact page if that's something you'd value.
faq_q27: I've found a bug or an incorrect recipe. What should I do?
faq_a27: Thanks for flagging it — accuracy matters to us. Visit the Contact page, choose 'Tech Help', and include the recipe name, what's wrong (wrong ingredient, allergy mis-tag, pricing issue, etc.), and your browser name and version if it's a technical glitch. A screenshot helps enormously. Allergy-related errors are prioritised and typically fixed within 48 hours; other corrections within a week.
faq_q28: What data do you store, and is it secure?
faq_a28: If you create an account, we store your email, saved plans, and favourites on Supabase — a SOC 2-compliant platform. We never store payment details, health records, or personal information about your children. Anonymous usage analytics (pages viewed, features used) go through Google Analytics with IP anonymisation turned on. We do not sell personal data to anyone. Full details are in our Privacy Policy.
faq_q29: Does the planner work without an internet connection?
faq_a29: Generating a plan requires internet because it queries our recipe database and pricing API in real time. Once the plan exists, download it as a PDF and you're set — the file works completely offline. A popular routine: generate on Sunday evening, save the PDF, and refer to it all week, no Wi-Fi needed.
faq_q30: How frequently do new recipes appear?
faq_a30: Roughly every month we publish 3–5 new recipes, each having passed through sourcing at Australian supermarkets, allergy verification, a test cook by a team member, and editorial review. Woolworths and Coles prices update every one to two weeks. At the start of each school term (Terms 1–4, late January to mid-December) we also run a seasonal refresh to ensure the recipe mix reflects what's currently in stock and well-priced.

policies_subtitle: How we handle your data and keep your family's information safe.
privacy_intro: Aussie Lunchbox Planner respects your privacy and handles personal information in accordance with the Australian Privacy Act 1988 (Cth) and the Australian Privacy Principles (APPs). This policy outlines what we collect, why, and how we keep it safe.
privacy_1_li1: If you sign in with Google, we receive your display name and email address — nothing more.
privacy_1_li2: Anonymous, aggregated statistics such as pages visited, features used, and session duration help us improve the planner. No individual browsing profile is created.
privacy_1_li3: Small data files store your language preference, dietary filters, and login session so you don't have to re-enter them on every visit.
privacy_2_li1: Operating and maintaining the planner, including saving your plans and favourites.
privacy_2_li2: Tailoring meal suggestions to the dietary filters and preferences you set.
privacy_2_li3: Sending transactional emails you explicitly request (e.g., emailing a plan to yourself).
privacy_3_p: Advertisements displayed on this site are served by Google AdSense, which may use cookies (including the DoubleClick cookie) to show ads relevant to your browsing history. You can manage or opt out of personalised ads at
privacy_3_mid: . Google's full data-use policy is available at
privacy_4_p: We run Google Analytics 4 with IP anonymisation enabled to gather aggregate usage patterns — such as which pages are visited most and average session length. No personally identifiable information is sent to Google. You can block analytics collection entirely via the
privacy_5_p: Authentication and database storage are handled by Supabase (SOC 2-compliant). Transactional emails are sent through Resend. Both services process data under their own privacy policies. We do not sell, rent, or trade your personal information with any third party.
privacy_6_p: We employ industry-standard security practices — encrypted connections (TLS), secure authentication tokens, and access controls — to protect the data we hold. While no system is entirely immune to risk, we continuously review and strengthen our safeguards.
privacy_7_p: For questions, access requests, or complaints regarding this Privacy Policy, reach us at
disclaimer_warning: Important Notice: Aussie Lunchbox Planner is a meal-planning and grocery-organisation tool. Nothing on this site constitutes medical, dietetic, or clinical nutrition advice. All content is provided for general informational purposes in line with guidance published by FSANZ (Food Standards Australia New Zealand).
disclaimer_1_p: Before making significant changes to your child's diet — particularly for medical conditions such as coeliac disease, diabetes, or food protein-induced conditions — seek guidance from your GP or an Accredited Practising Dietitian.
disclaimer_2_li1: Our allergen filters remove recipes that list specific allergens as ingredients. They do not account for trace contamination during commercial manufacturing.
disclaimer_2_li2: Product formulations, supplier sources, and production lines can change at any time without public notice.
disclaimer_2_li3: Read the label on every product you purchase — every single time — regardless of past experience with that item.
disclaimer_2_li4: Safe food handling in your own kitchen, including preventing cross-contamination between allergen-free and allergen-containing foods, remains your responsibility.
disclaimer_3_li1: Nutritional estimates are derived from the Australian Food Composition Database (FSANZ) and reflect standard serve sizes. They are approximations, not clinical measurements.
disclaimer_3_li2: Supermarket pricing is sourced from Woolworths and Coles and may fluctuate due to promotions, regional differences, or stock changes without prior notice.
disclaimer_footer: By continuing to use Aussie Lunchbox Planner, you confirm that you have read this disclaimer, understand its limitations, and accept full responsibility for how you apply the information provided.
cookie_message: Aussie Lunchbox uses cookies for site functionality and to show personalised ads through Google AdSense. Tap "Accept" to continue or "Decline" to browse without cookies.

home_usecase2_desc: One child is nut-free, the other is dairy-free? Stack filters and the planner handles both simultaneously. No more preparing two completely separate lunches.
home_usecase3_desc: With live Woolworths pricing and Coles comparisons, you see exactly where your money goes. Families tell us they typically spend $12-$18 for five days of healthy, home-made lunches.

home_commitment_heading: Backed by Science, Tested by Parents
home_commitment_desc: Every recipe on Aussie Lunchbox is developed following NHMRC Australian Dietary Guidelines and reviewed by our team of parent contributors. We test each meal with Australian supermarket ingredients, verify prices at Woolworths and Coles, and update our database every month. Our editorial standards are transparent — read them on our About page.
about_founding_story_continued: Today the team spans Sydney, Melbourne, and Brisbane — a handful of parents, a couple of writers, and one person who genuinely enjoys comparing Woolworths and Coles prices. Every recipe is cooked in a real kitchen before it goes live. Every price is verified at a real checkout. Every allergy tag is checked against the actual product label, not just the database entry.
share_plan_text: Here's my family's lunchbox plan for the week — made with Aussie Lunchbox!
planner_restored: Your last plan has been restored.
planner_seo_title: Build Your Family's Weekly Lunch Roster
planner_seo_intro: Aussie Lunchbox is a free, AI-powered tool that builds a personalised Monday-to-Friday school lunch plan for Australian families. Set your allergy filters, hit Generate, and within seconds you'll have five days of balanced meals — complete with recipes, nutritional estimates aligned to NHMRC guidelines, and a shopping list showing live Woolworths prices alongside Coles estimates. No sign-up required, no hidden fees. Just open the planner and get sorted.
planner_seo_step1_text: Toggle allergy filters (nut-free, dairy-free, gluten-free, egg-free) or dietary modes (vegetarian, vegan). Exclude any ingredients your child refuses and add leftovers from the fridge you'd like to use up first.
planner_seo_step2_name: Let the AI Do the Work
planner_seo_step2_text: Press 'Generate Plan' and within five seconds you'll see a balanced 5-day menu. Every day features a main lunch, a snack idea, a full ingredient list with quantities, step-by-step cooking instructions, and estimated calories.
planner_seo_step3_text: Export the whole plan as a PDF, fire it to your inbox, or share it over WhatsApp or Facebook. The shopping list is pre-sorted by supermarket aisle and shows Woolworths and Coles prices — ready to take straight to the checkout.
planner_seo_allergy_p: Our library of 100+ Australian-tested recipes carries detailed allergen tags verified against real product labels. When you activate a filter, only recipes that are genuinely free of that allergen appear in your plan. Whether you're managing peanut anaphylaxis, dairy intolerance, or coeliac-safe gluten-free requirements, the planner puts your child's safety front and centre.
planner_seo_price_h: Woolworths & Coles Prices, Side by Side
planner_seo_price_p: Every ingredient in your shopping list displays a live Woolworths price and a Coles estimate, so you can compare before you leave the house. We show per-portion costs, not just packet prices, giving you a realistic view of what the week will actually cost. Most Aussie families prepare five days of nutritious school lunches for under $20.
planner_seo_howto_name: How to Build a Weekly School Lunch Plan
planner_seo_howto_desc: Open Aussie Lunchbox, set your allergy and dietary filters, and generate a personalised 5-day school lunch roster with recipes, a categorised shopping list, and live Australian supermarket pricing.
home_guide_heading: Australian School Lunch Rules You Should Know
home_guide_rules_p1: Australian school food policies vary by state and even by individual school. Most primary schools enforce strict nut-free or nut-aware policies to protect children with anaphylaxis. This typically means no peanut butter, Nutella, satay sauce, or any products containing tree nuts. Some schools extend bans to sesame seeds and kiwi fruit. Always check your school's specific policy at enrolment and again at the start of each year — policies can change.
home_guide_rules_p2: The 'Nude Food' or 'Waste-Free' movement is widespread across Australian schools, encouraging families to pack lunches without disposable packaging. Use reusable containers, beeswax wraps, and silicone bags instead of cling film and zip-lock bags. Many schools also restrict sugary drinks — water bottles are expected in most classrooms. Aussie Lunchbox plans naturally align with these policies: meals focus on whole foods, minimal packaging, and no high-sugar processed snacks.
home_guide_nsw_desc: Healthy School Canteens Strategy uses a traffic-light system. Green = everyday foods, Amber = select carefully, Red = occasional only.
home_guide_vic_desc: School Canteens and Other School Food Services Policy emphasises the Australian Dietary Guidelines for all food sold at school.
home_guide_qld_desc: Smart Choices strategy categorises foods as Green (best), Amber (moderate), and Red (limit). Extra focus on hydration in tropical climates.
home_guide_wa_desc: Healthy Food and Drink in Public Schools policy mandates that 60% of food items sold must be 'green' category.
home_guide_nutrition_h: NHMRC Nutrition Guidelines for School-Age Children
home_guide_nutrition_p1: The National Health and Medical Research Council (NHMRC) publishes the Australian Dietary Guidelines, which recommend that children aged 4-8 consume approximately 1,400-1,600 calories per day, and children aged 9-13 need 1,800-2,200 calories. A school lunch should provide roughly 25-30% of daily energy — that's 400-500 calories for younger children and 500-650 for older primary students.
home_guide_nutrition_p2: The guidelines recommend five food groups at every meal: grains (preferably wholegrain), vegetables and legumes, fruit, lean meats and alternatives, and dairy. A well-packed lunchbox should include a protein source (chicken, eggs, cheese, or legumes), a complex carbohydrate (wholemeal bread, pasta, or rice), at least one serve of vegetables, and a piece of fruit. Aussie Lunchbox calculates estimated nutrition for every generated meal plan — calories, protein, carbs, and fats — so you can verify your child is getting adequate fuel for the school day.
home_tips_heading: Make the Most of Aussie Lunchbox
home_tips_seasonal_h: Plan Around the Australian School Calendar
home_tips_seasonal_p: Australia's school year runs from late January to mid-December across four terms. Term 1 (January-March) starts in peak summer — pack cold lunches with ice packs and avoid mayonnaise-based foods that spoil quickly. Term 2 (April-June) brings cooler weather — perfect for thermos soups, warm pasta, and baked goods. Term 3 (July-September) is winter in southern states but still mild up north — adjust your plans based on your location. Term 4 (October-December) heats up again as summer returns. Use Aussie Lunchbox's seasonal filters and blog guides to adapt your plans throughout the year.
home_tips_compare_h: Woolworths vs Coles: Use the Price Comparison
home_tips_compare_p: Every ingredient in your shopping list shows a price estimate. Prices marked 'Live' are fetched in real-time from Woolworths Australia's online catalogue, while 'Est.' prices are estimates based on historical data. We show portion costs — the actual amount you'll use per meal — not the full packet price. Tip: generate your plan on Thursday evening when new weekly specials drop at both Woolworths and Coles. Many parents find they save $5-$10 per week by timing their shop to coincide with catalogue specials on lunchbox staples like bread, cheese, and deli meats.
home_tips_family_p: For multi-allergy households, stack allergy filters to accommodate different children simultaneously — the planner generates a single meal plan that's safe for everyone. For budget-conscious families, browse our Budget category blog posts for batch-cooking strategies and tips on making a whole chicken stretch across three days of lunches. For picky eaters, use the ingredient exclusion feature and gradually introduce one new food per week alongside familiar favourites. And for families with children at different schools with different policies, generate separate plans with different filter settings — it takes just 10 seconds per plan.
